$comment: GENERATED from corpus-tools/src/oigt_corpus_tools/schema/model.py — do not edit by hand; run 'uv run oigt-corpus schema emit-meta' to regenerate.
title: OpenIGTLink Message Schema
description: Meta-schema that validates the shape of every file under spec/schemas/. Each message-type schema describes one OpenIGTLink wire message and is consumed by codegen tools across all language ports.

Endianness.description: Byte order for a multi-byte field on the wire.
Endianness.title: Endianness

ExtendedHeader.description: V3 extended-header layout, if non-default for a message type.

required.description: Whether the v3 extended header is required for this message type.
required.title: Required

ExtendedHeader.title: ExtendedHeader

FieldSchema.description: One field in a message body.

name.description: Field name, snake_case. Used as-is in codegen; language ports may apply idiomatic casing conversions.
name.title: Name

type.description: Field type. Primitives are encoded in the order declared. Composite types require additional attributes (``size_bytes``, ``count``, ``element_type``, ``length_prefix_type``, ``encoding``, etc.). ``trailing_string`` consumes all remaining body bytes and MUST be the last field in its message.
type.title: Type

description.description: Concise description of the field's meaning. Required.
description.title: Description

rationale.description: Optional. Why this field is shaped this way.
rationale.title: Rationale

introduced_in.description: Protocol version in which this field first appeared, if different from the message's ``introduced_in``.

endianness.description: Byte order. Defaults to big-endian (OpenIGTLink convention). Rarely overridden; if overridden, explain in ``rationale``.

size_bytes.description: Size in bytes on the wire. Required for ``fixed_string`` and ``fixed_bytes``; optional for other types if derivable from ``type`` alone.
size_bytes.title: Size Bytes

count.description: For ``array`` / ``struct_array``: either a fixed integer count, or the name of a sibling field that carries the count.
count.title: Count

element_type.description: For ``array``: the element's primitive type. For ``struct_array``: the name of an inline struct (not yet supported).
element_type.title: Element Type

layout.description: Optional semantic-layout hint (e.g. 'column_major_3x4' for TRANSFORM's matrix). Codegen may use this for helper methods; it has no effect on wire encoding.
layout.title: Layout

length_prefix_type.description: For ``length_prefixed_string``: the integer type used to encode the length prefix.

encoding.description: For string types: the expected text encoding.

null_padded.description: For ``fixed_string``: whether values shorter than ``size_bytes`` are padded with null bytes (true) or the full region is treated as the string (false). Defaults to true.
null_padded.title: Null Padded

null_terminated.description: For ``trailing_string``: whether the final byte on the wire MUST be 0x00. When true, the null is part of the field bytes (a non-empty string occupies ``len+1`` bytes) and the string value is the bytes excluding the final null. Defaults to false.
null_terminated.title: Null Terminated

legacy_notes.description: Historical quirks for this field that must be preserved for wire compatibility.
legacy_notes.title: Legacy Notes

notes.description: Other prose notes that do not fit ``rationale`` or ``legacy_notes``.
notes.title: Notes

FieldSchema.title: FieldSchema

FieldType.description:
Composite field types — everything that isn't a numeric primitive.

``trailing_string`` consumes all remaining body bytes and MUST be the
last field in its message. ``length_prefixed_string`` requires a
``length_prefix_type``. ``fixed_string`` requires ``size_bytes``.
FieldType.title: FieldType

PrimitiveType.description: Fixed-width numeric types that can appear directly in a message body.
PrimitiveType.title: PrimitiveType

ProtocolVersion.description: OpenIGTLink protocol version in which a feature first appeared.
ProtocolVersion.title: ProtocolVersion

SpecReference.description: Back-reference from a schema or field into the prose spec document.

section.description: Section identifier in the prose spec document (e.g. '4.3.2' or 'Body (STATUS)').
section.title: Section

document.description: Prose spec document this entry references (e.g. 'protocol/v3.md'). Defaults to the document matching ``introduced_in``.
document.title: Document

url.description: Optional canonical URL for the spec section.
url.title: Url

SpecReference.title: SpecReference

message_type.description: Canonical logical name for this message type (e.g. 'TRANSFORM'). Used to construct language-native type names during codegen.
message_type.title: Message Type

type_id.description: Wire type identifier. Written as a 12-byte ASCII field, null-padded on the right. The string value here contains the significant bytes only; padding is applied during encoding.
type_id.title: Type Id

introduced_in.description: Protocol version in which this message type was first defined.

deprecated_in.description: Protocol version in which this message type was deprecated, if any. Deprecated types must still round-trip correctly for wire compatibility.
deprecated_in.title: Deprecated In

description.description: Concise human-readable summary. Required. Flows into every language port's generated API documentation.
description.title: Description

rationale.description: Optional prose explaining why the message type or its wire layout is shaped this way. Historical or design-decision context, not behavioral contract.
rationale.title: Rationale

spec_reference.description: Back-reference into the prose spec document.

body_size.description: Total body size in bytes if fixed, or the string 'variable' if determined by the fields at encode time.
body_size.title: Body Size

fields.description: Ordered list of fields in the message body. Fields are parsed in order.
fields.title: Fields

extended_header.description: V3 extended-header layout for this message type, if non-default.

metadata_allowed.description: Whether this message type accepts a trailing metadata block in v2/v3. Defaults to true.
metadata_allowed.title: Metadata Allowed

legacy_notes.description: Historical quirks that must be preserved for wire compatibility. These are contract, not documentation — every entry represents a decision that a new implementation must match. Prefix with a version tag where relevant (e.g. 'v2.1:').
legacy_notes.title: Legacy Notes

notes.description: Other prose notes that do not fit rationale, spec_reference, or legacy_notes.
notes.title: Notes

see_also.description: Logical names of related message types.
see_also.title: See Also
